52	    ├── agents/             # Agent definitions (engineer, TA, PM, etc.)
53	    └── commands/           # Team commands (start-feature, start-review)
54	```
55	
56	### CLI Directory Detail (`/cli/src/`)
57	
58	- `commands/` — CLI commands (init, sync, status, dashboard, reset, install-hook, config, reflect, telemetry, insights)
59	- `commands/stats/` — Stats command suite (4-layer architecture):
60	  - `data/types.ts` — `StatsDataSource` interface, `SessionRow`, error classes
61	  - `data/source.ts` — Data source factory
62	  - `data/local.ts` — SQLite data source implementation
63	  - `data/aggregation.ts` — Pure compute functions (overview, cost, projects, today, models)
64	  - `data/fuzzy-match.ts` — Levenshtein distance for `--project` name matching
65	  - `render/` — Terminal rendering (colors, format, charts, layout)
66	  - `actions/` — Action handlers for each subcommand + shared error handler
67	  - `index.ts` — Command tree with lazy imports
68	  - `shared.ts` — Shared CLI flags
69	- `providers/` — Source tool providers (claude-code, cursor, codex, copilot, copilot-cli)
70	- `providers/types.ts` — `SessionProvider` interface
71	- `providers/registry.ts` — Provider registration and lookup
72	- `parser/jsonl.ts` — JSONL file parsing (used by ClaudeCodeProvider)
73	- `parser/titles.ts` — Smart session title generation (5-tier fallback strategy)
74	- `db/` — SQLite schema, migrations, query functions
75	- `utils/config.ts` — Configuration management (~/.code-insights/config.json)
76	- `utils/device.ts` — Device ID generation, git remote detection, stable project IDs
77	- `utils/paths.ts` — Virtual path handling (shared by sync and stats)
78	- `utils/telemetry.ts` — PostHog telemetry (opt-out model, 14 event types)
79	- `types.ts` — TypeScript type definitions (SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH)
80	- `index.ts` — CLI entry point (Commander.js)
81	
82	---
83	
84	## Provider Architecture
85	
86	All source tools are integrated via the `SessionProvider` interface (`providers/types.ts`):
87	
88	```typescript
89	interface SessionProvider {
90	  getProviderName(): string;                                    // e.g. 'claude-code', 'cursor'
91	  discover(options?: { projectFilter?: string }): Promise<string[]>;  // Find session files
92	  parse(filePath: string): Promise<ParsedSession | null>;       // Parse into common format
93	}
94	```
95	
96	Providers are registered in `providers/registry.ts`. To add a new source tool:
97	1. Create `providers/<name>.ts` implementing `SessionProvider`
98	2. Register it in `providers/registry.ts`
99	3. Add color entry to dashboard `SOURCE_TOOL_COLORS`
100	4. Add avatar case to dashboard `getAssistantConfig()`
101	5. Add tool name aliases if tool names differ
102	6. Add option to source filter dropdown
103	
104	---
105	
106	## SQLite Database
107	
108	- **Location:** `~/.code-insights/data.db`
109	- **Mode:** WAL (concurrent reads during CLI sync)
110	- **Driver:** better-sqlite3 (synchronous, fast, no async overhead)
111	- **Schema:** Versioned migrations (V1–V7) applied on startup
112	- **Timestamps:** ISO 8601 strings
113	
114	### Tables
115	
116	| Table | Purpose | Schema Version |
117	|-------|---------|---------------|
118	| `projects` | Project metadata (id = hash of git remote URL or path) | V1 |
119	| `sessions` | Session metadata, titles, character classification, `deleted_at` soft-delete; V6 adds `compact_count INTEGER`, `auto_compact_count INTEGER`, `slash_commands TEXT` | V1, V5, V6 |
120	| `messages` | Full message content (stored during sync) | V1 |
121	| `insights` | LLM-generated insights (5 types) | V1, V2 (index) |
122	| `usage_stats` | Global usage aggregation | V1 |
123	| `session_facets` | Cross-session facet data (friction, patterns, workflow) | V3 |
124	| `reflect_snapshots` | Cached synthesis results, composite PK `(period, project_id, source_tool)` | V4 |
125	| `analysis_usage` | Per-session LLM analysis cost data, composite PK `(session_id, analysis_type)` | V7 |
126	| `schema_version` | Migration tracking | V1 |
127	
128	---
129	
130	## Type Architecture (CRITICAL)
131	
132	Types are defined **once** in `cli/src/types.ts`. This is the single source of truth for the entire monorepo.
133	
134	```
135	CLI (cli/src/types.ts)       -> Writes to SQLite
136	Server (server/src/)         -> Reads from SQLite, exposes via API
137	Dashboard (dashboard/src/)   -> Reads from Server API
138	```
139	
140	**Rules:**
141	- New SQLite columns MUST have defaults or be nullable (backward compatible)
142	- Type changes in `types.ts` must be reflected in SQLite migrations
143	- TA owns this contract — flag all type changes to `technical-architect`
144	
145	### Key Types (`cli/src/types.ts`)
146	
147	| Type | Purpose |
148	|------|---------|
149	| `ClaudeMessage` | Individual message entry |
150	| `ParsedSession` | Aggregated session with metadata, title, character |
151	| `Insight` | Types: summary, decision, learning, technique, prompt_quality; source: 'llm' |
152	| `FrictionPoint` | Friction with category, severity, resolution, description; optional `attribution` field (`'user-actionable' \| 'ai-capability' \| 'environmental'`) |
153	| `EffectivePattern` | Pattern with required `category`, `description`, `confidence`; optional `driver` field (`'user-driven' \| 'ai-driven' \| 'collaborative'`); CoT `_reasoning` scratchpad stored in JSON blob |
154	| `SessionCharacter` | 7 classifications: deep_focus, bug_hunt, feature_build, exploration, refactor, learning, quick_task |
155	| `ClaudeInsightConfig` | Config format |
156	| `PQDimensionScores` | Per-dimension PQ averages (overall, context_provision, request_specificity, scope_management, information_timing, correction_quality); used by share card |
157	| `SyncState` | File modification tracking for incremental sync |
158	
159	### Friction & Pattern Normalization
160	
161	Both friction points and effective patterns use canonical category taxonomies with Levenshtein-based normalization (`server/src/llm/friction-normalize.ts`, `server/src/llm/pattern-normalize.ts`).
162	
163	**Friction categories (9 canonical):** `wrong-approach`, `knowledge-gap`, `stale-assumptions`, `incomplete-requirements`, `context-loss`, `scope-creep`, `repeated-mistakes`, `documentation-gap`, `tooling-limitation`
164	
165	**Effective pattern categories (8 canonical):** `structured-planning`, `incremental-implementation`, `verification-workflow`, `systematic-debugging`, `self-correction`, `context-gathering`, `domain-expertise`, `effective-tooling`
166	
167	**Normalization pipeline:** exact match → alias lookup → Levenshtein (distance ≤ 2) → substring match → pass-through (novel category). Normalization runs at write time in `saveFacetsToDb()` and at read time as a belt-and-suspenders guard.
168	
169	**Legacy alias remapping:** 11 old friction categories (from the original 15-category taxonomy) are aliased to the current 9. See `FRICTION_ALIASES` in `friction-normalize.ts`.
170	
171	**Attribution model:** Each friction point carries an optional `attribution` field classifying who contributed: `user-actionable` (better input would have prevented it), `ai-capability` (AI failed despite adequate input), or `environmental` (external constraint). Old data without attribution is detected by the `/api/facets/outdated` endpoint.
172	
173	---
174	
175	## Server API Routes
176	
177	### Core Resources
178	
179	| Route | Method | Purpose |
180	|-------|--------|---------|
181	| `/api/health` | GET | Server health check |
182	| `/api/projects` | GET | List all projects |
183	| `/api/projects/:id` | GET | Project detail |
184	| `/api/sessions` | GET | Session list with filters |
185	| `/api/sessions/:id` | GET | Session detail |
186	| `/api/sessions/:id` | PATCH | Update session (custom title, soft delete) |
187	| `/api/sessions/:id` | DELETE | Soft-delete a session |
188	| `/api/sessions/deleted/count` | GET | Count of soft-deleted sessions |
189	| `/api/messages/:sessionId` | GET | Message content for a session |
190	| `/api/insights` | GET | Browse generated insights |
191	| `/api/insights` | POST | Create an insight |
192	| `/api/insights/:id` | DELETE | Delete an insight |
193	
194	### Analytics & Stats
195	
196	| Route | Method | Purpose |
197	|-------|--------|---------|
198	| `/api/analytics/dashboard` | GET | Analytics overview aggregation |
199	| `/api/analytics/usage` | GET | Global usage stats |
200	
201	### Analysis (LLM-Powered)
202	
203	| Route | Method | Purpose |
204	|-------|--------|---------|
205	| `/api/analysis/usage` | GET | Analysis cost/usage data per session |
206	| `/api/analysis/session` | POST | Trigger session analysis with LLM |
207	| `/api/analysis/session/stream` | GET | SSE streaming for session analysis |
208	| `/api/analysis/prompt-quality` | POST | Trigger prompt quality analysis |
209	| `/api/analysis/prompt-quality/stream` | GET | SSE streaming for PQ analysis |
210	| `/api/analysis/recurring` | POST | Find recurring insight patterns |
211	
212	### Export
213	
214	| Route | Method | Purpose |
215	|-------|--------|---------|
216	| `/api/export/markdown` | POST | Session-level markdown export (Knowledge Base / Agent Rules templates) |
217	| `/api/export/generate` | POST | LLM-powered cross-session export synthesis |
218	| `/api/export/generate/stream` | GET | SSE streaming for export generation |
219	
220	### Facets
221	
222	| Route | Method | Purpose |
223	|-------|--------|---------|
224	| `/api/facets` | GET | Session facets data |
225	| `/api/facets/aggregated` | GET | Pre-aggregated friction/patterns |
226	| `/api/facets/missing` | GET | Sessions with insights but no facets |
227	| `/api/facets/outdated` | GET | Sessions missing `effective_patterns.category`/`driver` or `friction_points.attribution` |
228	| `/api/facets/backfill` | POST | Backfill facets for legacy sessions (`force` option) |
229	| `/api/facets/missing-pq` | GET | Sessions missing prompt quality analysis |
230	| `/api/facets/outdated-pq` | GET | Sessions with outdated prompt quality insights |
231	| `/api/facets/backfill-pq` | POST | Backfill prompt quality for sessions |
232	
233	### Reflect (Cross-Session Synthesis)
234	
235	| Route | Method | Purpose |
236	|-------|--------|---------|
237	| `/api/reflect/generate` | POST | Cross-session LLM synthesis (SSE streaming) |
238	| `/api/reflect/results` | GET | Aggregated facet data without LLM synthesis |
239	| `/api/reflect/weeks` | GET | Last 8 ISO weeks with session counts and snapshot status |
240	| `/api/reflect/snapshot` | GET | Cached synthesis snapshot for a specific week/project |
241	
242	### Configuration & Telemetry
243	
244	| Route | Method | Purpose |
245	|-------|--------|---------|
246	| `/api/config/llm` | GET | Current LLM configuration |
247	| `/api/config/llm` | PUT | Update LLM configuration |
248	| `/api/config/llm/test` | POST | Test LLM credentials |
249	| `/api/config/llm/ollama-models` | GET | Discover available Ollama models |
250	| `/api/telemetry/identity` | GET | Telemetry identity and opt-out status |
251	
252	---
253	
254	## Dashboard Pages
255	
256	| Page | Route | Purpose |
257	|------|-------|---------|
258	| Dashboard | `/dashboard` | Overview with charts (`/` redirects here) |
259	| Sessions | `/sessions` | Session list with filters |
260	| Session Detail | `/sessions/:id` | Full session with analyze button |
261	| Insights | `/insights` | Browse generated insights |
262	| Analytics | `/analytics` | Charts: cost, models, projects |
263	| Patterns | `/patterns` | Cross-session synthesis (Friction & Wins, Rules & Skills, Working Style) |
264	| Export | `/export` | LLM-powered export wizard (4 formats, 3 depths) |
265	| Journal | `/journal` | Chronological timeline of learnings and decisions by ISO week |
266	| Settings | `/settings` | Configuration UI |
267	
268	---
269	
270	## Share Card Pipeline
271	
272	The share card generates a 1200×630 PNG (OG image standard) from Canvas 2D:
273	
274	```
275	PatternsPage → useFacetAggregation(period) → WeekAtAGlanceStrip → "Share" button
276	                                                                       ↓
277	                                              downloadShareCard() → drawShareCard()
278	                                                                       ↓
279	                                              Canvas 2D (2400×1260 @ 2× DPR) → toBlob() → PNG download
280	```
281	
282	**Data sources for the card:**
283	- `computePQScores()` in `server/src/routes/shared-aggregation.ts` — 4-week rolling PQ dimension averages
284	- Working-style tagline from Reflect LLM synthesis
285	- Effective patterns from facet aggregation (top 3 by frequency)
286	- Lifetime session count (all-time, no date filter)
287	- Token sum from 4-week scoring window
288	- Source tools from sessions in scope
289	
290	**Key files:**
291	- `dashboard/src/lib/share-card-utils.ts` — Canvas 2D drawing logic (`drawShareCard()`, `downloadShareCard()`)
292	- `dashboard/src/lib/share-card-icons.ts` — Lucide icon + tool logo rendering (`drawIcon()`, `drawToolIcon()`)
293	- `dashboard/src/components/patterns/WeekAtAGlanceStrip.tsx` — UI component with download trigger
294	- `dashboard/public/icons/` — Static tool logo assets (SVG/PNG)
295	
296	---
297	
298	## Known Architectural Debt
299	
300	Items identified during the production-grade audit (2026-03-21) and intentionally deferred. Revisit when their trigger conditions are met.
301	
302	| Item | File | Trigger | Notes |
303	|------|------|---------|-------|
304	| Refactor `AnalysisContext` | `dashboard/src/components/analysis/AnalysisContext.tsx` (256 lines) | When parallel/concurrent analyses are needed | Currently mixes SSE streaming orchestration with React state management. Works correctly as a single-analysis state machine. Refactor into separate streaming hook + read-only context when concurrent analysis support is required. |
305	| Split `route-helpers.ts` | `server/src/routes/route-helpers.ts` (353 lines) | When SSE protocol or middleware evolves independently | 3 cohesive concerns (DB loading, middleware, SSE) always co-imported. Split only if concerns diverge. |
306	| Remaining `console.warn` monitors | `server/src/llm/response-parsers.ts` | After confirming classification quality is stable | 4 remaining monitors (`[friction-monitor]`, `[pattern-monitor]`) — add env toggle or remove once confident in LLM output quality. |
307

# Architecture — Code Insights

> Technical architecture reference. Linked from [CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md).

---

## Data Flow

```
Source tool session files -> Provider (discover + parse) -> SQLite -> Dashboard (localhost:7890)
                                                         -> CLI stats commands
```

---

## Repository Structure

```
code-insights/
├── cli/                    # Node.js CLI (Commander.js, SQLite, providers)
│   └── src/
│       ├── commands/       # CLI commands (init, sync, status, stats, dashboard, config, insights)
│       ├── commands/stats/ # Stats command suite (4-layer architecture)
│       ├── analysis/       # Prompt builders, response parsers, normalizers, runner interface (shared by CLI + server)
│       ├── providers/      # Source tool providers (claude-code, cursor, codex, copilot, copilot-cli)
│       ├── parser/         # JSONL parsing, title generation
│       ├── db/             # SQLite schema, migrations, queries
│       ├── utils/          # Config, device, paths, telemetry
│       ├── types.ts        # Type definitions (SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH)
│       └── index.ts        # CLI entry point
├── dashboard/              # Vite + React SPA
│   └── src/
│       ├── components/     # React components (shadcn/ui)
│       │   ├── empty-states/  # Guided empty states (EmptyDashboard, EmptySessions, EmptyInsights)
│       │   └── patterns/      # Patterns page components (WeekAtAGlanceStrip, WeekSelector)
│       ├── hooks/          # React Query hooks
│       ├── lib/            # LLM providers, utilities, telemetry
│       │   ├── share-card-utils.ts   # Canvas 2D share card rendering (drawShareCard, downloadShareCard)
│       │   ├── share-card-icons.ts   # Lucide icon + tool logo rendering for Canvas 2D
│       │   └── prompt-quality-utils.ts  # PQ category labels, strength set detection
│       └── App.tsx         # SPA entry point
│   └── public/
│       └── icons/          # Source tool logos (Claude Code SVG, Cursor PNG, Codex PNG, Copilot PNG)
├── server/                 # Hono API server
│   └── src/
│       ├── routes/         # REST API endpoints
│       ├── llm/            # LLM client, reflect synthesis, export, cost tracking; prompt builders re-exported from cli/src/analysis/
│       └── index.ts        # Server entry point
├── docs/                   # Product docs, plans, roadmap
│   └── plans/              # Design plans (pending implementation only)
└── .claude/                # Agent definitions, commands, hookify rules
    ├── agents/             # Agent definitions (engineer, TA, PM, etc.)
    └── commands/           # Team commands (start-feature, start-review)
```

### CLI Directory Detail (`/cli/src/`)

- `commands/` — CLI commands (init, sync, status, dashboard, reset, install-hook, config, reflect, telemetry, insights)
- `commands/stats/` — Stats command suite (4-layer architecture):
  - `data/types.ts` — `StatsDataSource` interface, `SessionRow`, error classes
  - `data/source.ts` — Data source factory
  - `data/local.ts` — SQLite data source implementation
  - `data/aggregation.ts` — Pure compute functions (overview, cost, projects, today, models)
  - `data/fuzzy-match.ts` — Levenshtein distance for `--project` name matching
  - `render/` — Terminal rendering (colors, format, charts, layout)
  - `actions/` — Action handlers for each subcommand + shared error handler
  - `index.ts` — Command tree with lazy imports
  - `shared.ts` — Shared CLI flags
- `providers/` — Source tool providers (claude-code, cursor, codex, copilot, copilot-cli)
- `providers/types.ts` — `SessionProvider` interface
- `providers/registry.ts` — Provider registration and lookup
- `parser/jsonl.ts` — JSONL file parsing (used by ClaudeCodeProvider)
- `parser/titles.ts` — Smart session title generation (5-tier fallback strategy)
- `db/` — SQLite schema, migrations, query functions
- `utils/config.ts` — Configuration management (~/.code-insights/config.json)
- `utils/device.ts` — Device ID generation, git remote detection, stable project IDs
- `utils/paths.ts` — Virtual path handling (shared by sync and stats)
- `utils/telemetry.ts` — PostHog telemetry (opt-out model, 14 event types)
- `types.ts` — TypeScript type definitions (SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH)
- `index.ts` — CLI entry point (Commander.js)

---

## Provider Architecture

All source tools are integrated via the `SessionProvider` interface (`providers/types.ts`):

```typescript
interface SessionProvider {
  getProviderName(): string;                                    // e.g. 'claude-code', 'cursor'
  discover(options?: { projectFilter?: string }): Promise<string[]>;  // Find session files
  parse(filePath: string): Promise<ParsedSession | null>;       // Parse into common format
}
```

Providers are registered in `providers/registry.ts`. To add a new source tool:
1. Create `providers/<name>.ts` implementing `SessionProvider`
2. Register it in `providers/registry.ts`
3. Add color entry to dashboard `SOURCE_TOOL_COLORS`
4. Add avatar case to dashboard `getAssistantConfig()`
5. Add tool name aliases if tool names differ
6. Add option to source filter dropdown

---

## SQLite Database

- **Location:** `~/.code-insights/data.db`
- **Mode:** WAL (concurrent reads during CLI sync)
- **Driver:** better-sqlite3 (synchronous, fast, no async overhead)
- **Schema:** Versioned migrations (V1–V7) applied on startup
- **Timestamps:** ISO 8601 strings

### Tables

| Table | Purpose | Schema Version |
|-------|---------|---------------|
| `projects` | Project metadata (id = hash of git remote URL or path) | V1 |
| `sessions` | Session metadata, titles, character classification, `deleted_at` soft-delete; V6 adds `compact_count INTEGER`, `auto_compact_count INTEGER`, `slash_commands TEXT` | V1, V5, V6 |
| `messages` | Full message content (stored during sync) | V1 |
| `insights` | LLM-generated insights (5 types) | V1, V2 (index) |
| `usage_stats` | Global usage aggregation | V1 |
| `session_facets` | Cross-session facet data (friction, patterns, workflow) | V3 |
| `reflect_snapshots` | Cached synthesis results, composite PK `(period, project_id, source_tool)` | V4 |
| `analysis_usage` | Per-session LLM analysis cost data, composite PK `(session_id, analysis_type)` | V7 |
| `schema_version` | Migration tracking | V1 |

---

## Type Architecture (CRITICAL)

Types are defined **once** in `cli/src/types.ts`. This is the single source of truth for the entire monorepo.

```
CLI (cli/src/types.ts)       -> Writes to SQLite
Server (server/src/)         -> Reads from SQLite, exposes via API
Dashboard (dashboard/src/)   -> Reads from Server API
```

**Rules:**
- New SQLite columns MUST have defaults or be nullable (backward compatible)
- Type changes in `types.ts` must be reflected in SQLite migrations
- TA owns this contract — flag all type changes to `technical-architect`

### Key Types (`cli/src/types.ts`)

| Type | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `ClaudeMessage` | Individual message entry |
| `ParsedSession` | Aggregated session with metadata, title, character |
| `Insight` | Types: summary, decision, learning, technique, prompt_quality; source: 'llm' |
| `FrictionPoint` | Friction with category, severity, resolution, description; optional `attribution` field (`'user-actionable' \| 'ai-capability' \| 'environmental'`) |
| `EffectivePattern` | Pattern with required `category`, `description`, `confidence`; optional `driver` field (`'user-driven' \| 'ai-driven' \| 'collaborative'`); CoT `_reasoning` scratchpad stored in JSON blob |
| `SessionCharacter` | 7 classifications: deep_focus, bug_hunt, feature_build, exploration, refactor, learning, quick_task |
| `ClaudeInsightConfig` | Config format |
| `PQDimensionScores` | Per-dimension PQ averages (overall, context_provision, request_specificity, scope_management, information_timing, correction_quality); used by share card |
| `SyncState` | File modification tracking for incremental sync |

### Friction & Pattern Normalization

Both friction points and effective patterns use canonical category taxonomies with Levenshtein-based normalization (`server/src/llm/friction-normalize.ts`, `server/src/llm/pattern-normalize.ts`).

**Friction categories (9 canonical):** `wrong-approach`, `knowledge-gap`, `stale-assumptions`, `incomplete-requirements`, `context-loss`, `scope-creep`, `repeated-mistakes`, `documentation-gap`, `tooling-limitation`

**Effective pattern categories (8 canonical):** `structured-planning`, `incremental-implementation`, `verification-workflow`, `systematic-debugging`, `self-correction`, `context-gathering`, `domain-expertise`, `effective-tooling`

**Normalization pipeline:** exact match → alias lookup → Levenshtein (distance ≤ 2) → substring match → pass-through (novel category). Normalization runs at write time in `saveFacetsToDb()` and at read time as a belt-and-suspenders guard.

**Legacy alias remapping:** 11 old friction categories (from the original 15-category taxonomy) are aliased to the current 9. See `FRICTION_ALIASES` in `friction-normalize.ts`.

**Attribution model:** Each friction point carries an optional `attribution` field classifying who contributed: `user-actionable` (better input would have prevented it), `ai-capability` (AI failed despite adequate input), or `environmental` (external constraint). Old data without attribution is detected by the `/api/facets/outdated` endpoint.

---

## Server API Routes

### Core Resources

| Route | Method | Purpose |
|-------|--------|---------|
| `/api/health` | GET | Server health check |
| `/api/projects` | GET | List all projects |
| `/api/projects/:id` | GET | Project detail |
| `/api/sessions` | GET | Session list with filters |
| `/api/sessions/:id` | GET | Session detail |
| `/api/sessions/:id` | PATCH | Update session (custom title, soft delete) |
| `/api/sessions/:id` | DELETE | Soft-delete a session |
| `/api/sessions/deleted/count` | GET | Count of soft-deleted sessions |
| `/api/messages/:sessionId` | GET | Message content for a session |
| `/api/insights` | GET | Browse generated insights |
| `/api/insights` | POST | Create an insight |
| `/api/insights/:id` | DELETE | Delete an insight |

### Analytics & Stats

| Route | Method | Purpose |
|-------|--------|---------|
| `/api/analytics/dashboard` | GET | Analytics overview aggregation |
| `/api/analytics/usage` | GET | Global usage stats |

### Analysis (LLM-Powered)

| Route | Method | Purpose |
|-------|--------|---------|
| `/api/analysis/usage` | GET | Analysis cost/usage data per session |
| `/api/analysis/session` | POST | Trigger session analysis with LLM |
| `/api/analysis/session/stream` | GET | SSE streaming for session analysis |
| `/api/analysis/prompt-quality` | POST | Trigger prompt quality analysis |
| `/api/analysis/prompt-quality/stream` | GET | SSE streaming for PQ analysis |
| `/api/analysis/recurring` | POST | Find recurring insight patterns |

### Export

| Route | Method | Purpose |
|-------|--------|---------|
| `/api/export/markdown` | POST | Session-level markdown export (Knowledge Base / Agent Rules templates) |
| `/api/export/generate` | POST | LLM-powered cross-session export synthesis |
| `/api/export/generate/stream` | GET | SSE streaming for export generation |

### Facets

| Route | Method | Purpose |
|-------|--------|---------|
| `/api/facets` | GET | Session facets data |
| `/api/facets/aggregated` | GET | Pre-aggregated friction/patterns |
| `/api/facets/missing` | GET | Sessions with insights but no facets |
| `/api/facets/outdated` | GET | Sessions missing `effective_patterns.category`/`driver` or `friction_points.attribution` |
| `/api/facets/backfill` | POST | Backfill facets for legacy sessions (`force` option) |
| `/api/facets/missing-pq` | GET | Sessions missing prompt quality analysis |
| `/api/facets/outdated-pq` | GET | Sessions with outdated prompt quality insights |
| `/api/facets/backfill-pq` | POST | Backfill prompt quality for sessions |

### Reflect (Cross-Session Synthesis)

| Route | Method | Purpose |
|-------|--------|---------|
| `/api/reflect/generate` | POST | Cross-session LLM synthesis (SSE streaming) |
| `/api/reflect/results` | GET | Aggregated facet data without LLM synthesis |
| `/api/reflect/weeks` | GET | Last 8 ISO weeks with session counts and snapshot status |
| `/api/reflect/snapshot` | GET | Cached synthesis snapshot for a specific week/project |

### Configuration & Telemetry

| Route | Method | Purpose |
|-------|--------|---------|
| `/api/config/llm` | GET | Current LLM configuration |
| `/api/config/llm` | PUT | Update LLM configuration |
| `/api/config/llm/test` | POST | Test LLM credentials |
| `/api/config/llm/ollama-models` | GET | Discover available Ollama models |
| `/api/telemetry/identity` | GET | Telemetry identity and opt-out status |

---

## Dashboard Pages

| Page | Route | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| Dashboard | `/dashboard` | Overview with charts (`/` redirects here) |
| Sessions | `/sessions` | Session list with filters |
| Session Detail | `/sessions/:id` | Full session with analyze button |
| Insights | `/insights` | Browse generated insights |
| Analytics | `/analytics` | Charts: cost, models, projects |
| Patterns | `/patterns` | Cross-session synthesis (Friction & Wins, Rules & Skills, Working Style) |
| Export | `/export` | LLM-powered export wizard (4 formats, 3 depths) |
| Journal | `/journal` | Chronological timeline of learnings and decisions by ISO week |
| Settings | `/settings` | Configuration UI |

---

## Share Card Pipeline

The share card generates a 1200×630 PNG (OG image standard) from Canvas 2D:

```
PatternsPage → useFacetAggregation(period) → WeekAtAGlanceStrip → "Share" button
                                                                       ↓
                                              downloadShareCard() → drawShareCard()
                                                                       ↓
                                              Canvas 2D (2400×1260 @ 2× DPR) → toBlob() → PNG download
```

**Data sources for the card:**
- `computePQScores()` in `server/src/routes/shared-aggregation.ts` — 4-week rolling PQ dimension averages
- Working-style tagline from Reflect LLM synthesis
- Effective patterns from facet aggregation (top 3 by frequency)
- Lifetime session count (all-time, no date filter)
- Token sum from 4-week scoring window
- Source tools from sessions in scope

**Key files:**
- `dashboard/src/lib/share-card-utils.ts` — Canvas 2D drawing logic (`drawShareCard()`, `downloadShareCard()`)
- `dashboard/src/lib/share-card-icons.ts` — Lucide icon + tool logo rendering (`drawIcon()`, `drawToolIcon()`)
- `dashboard/src/components/patterns/WeekAtAGlanceStrip.tsx` — UI component with download trigger
- `dashboard/public/icons/` — Static tool logo assets (SVG/PNG)

---

## Known Architectural Debt

Items identified during the production-grade audit (2026-03-21) and intentionally deferred. Revisit when their trigger conditions are met.

| Item | File | Trigger | Notes |
|------|------|---------|-------|
| Refactor `AnalysisContext` | `dashboard/src/components/analysis/AnalysisContext.tsx` (256 lines) | When parallel/concurrent analyses are needed | Currently mixes SSE streaming orchestration with React state management. Works correctly as a single-analysis state machine. Refactor into separate streaming hook + read-only context when concurrent analysis support is required. |
| Split `route-helpers.ts` | `server/src/routes/route-helpers.ts` (353 lines) | When SSE protocol or middleware evolves independently | 3 cohesive concerns (DB loading, middleware, SSE) always co-imported. Split only if concerns diverge. |
| Remaining `console.warn` monitors | `server/src/llm/response-parsers.ts` | After confirming classification quality is stable | 4 remaining monitors (`[friction-monitor]`, `[pattern-monitor]`) — add env toggle or remove once confident in LLM output quality. |

1	# Development Practices — Code Insights
2	
3	> Development rules, branch discipline, and operational procedures. Linked from [CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md).
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Pre-Action Verification (CRITICAL)
8	
9	Before any state-modifying command (git checkout, git push, git tag, file edits), run a **read-only check** to verify current state:
10	
11	- **Branch names:** Never assume `main` vs `master` — run `git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD` to detect the default branch
12	- **File existence:** Read a file before editing it; `ls` a directory before writing to it
13	- **API signatures:** When calling an unfamiliar endpoint or tool, read the function signature or documentation first
14	- **Build context:** Before running build scripts, verify you're in the correct sub-directory (`pwd`) and on the correct branch (`git branch`)
15	
16	This applies to both **planning** and **execution**.
17	
18	---
19	
20	## Retry Discipline
21	
22	If a command or tool call fails twice on the same input, **STOP**. Do not retry a third time. Report the failure, state what was attempted, and propose an alternative approach.
23	
24	---
25	
26	## Branch Discipline (CRITICAL)
27	
28	`main` is the production branch. Only receives commits via merged PRs.
29	
30	**Rules for ALL agents:**
31	
32	```bash
33	# BEFORE ANY COMMIT:
34	git branch  # Must show feature branch, NOT main
35	
36	# If on main -> STOP:
37	git checkout -b feature/description
38	
39	# After EVERY commit:
40	git push origin $(git branch --show-current)  # Push IMMEDIATELY
41	```
42	
43	**Branch naming:**
44	- `feature/description` (new functionality)
45	- `fix/description` (bug fixes)
46	- `docs/description` (documentation only)
47	- `chore/description` (maintenance, deps, config)
48	
49	**Pre-commit checklist (ALL agents):**
50	1. `git branch` — Am I on feature branch?
51	2. If on `main` -> STOP, create feature branch
52	3. Commit to feature branch
53	4. Push immediately after commit
54	
55	---
56	
57	## Hookify Rules
58	
59	| Rule | Type | Purpose |
60	|------|------|---------|
61	| `block-pr-merge` | **block** | Agents never merge PRs — founder only |
62	| `block-local-merge-to-main` | **block** | Prevent `git merge` — all merges via GitHub PRs |
63	| `branch-discipline` | warn | Dev agents verify feature branch before coding |
64	| `agent-parallel-warning` | warn | Verify no dependencies before parallelizing agents |
65	| `no-jira` | **block** | Prevent Jira/Atlassian API calls — use GitHub Issues instead |
66	| `use-git-rm-for-tracked-files` | **block** | Enforce `git rm` over `rm` for source files |
67	| `review-before-pr` | warn | Remind: code review required before PR creation (bash path) |
68	| `review-before-pr-mcp` | warn | Remind: code review required before PR creation (MCP path) |
69	| `verify-before-checkout` | warn | Verify branch exists before `git checkout/switch` |
70	| `default-subagent-execution` | warn | Auto-answer: default to subagent-driven execution |
71	| `tdd-domain-check` | warn | On `git commit`, remind to include tests when touching TDD domains |
72	
73	**Native PreToolUse hooks** (in `settings.json`, not hookify):
74	
75	| Trigger | Purpose |
76	|---------|---------|
77	| `gh pr create` (Bash) | Run `pnpm build && pnpm test` — blocks PR creation on failure |
78	| `create_pull_request` (MCP) | Same CI gate for GitHub MCP tool path |
79	
80	---
81	
82	## TDD Strategy
83	
84	Code Insights uses **strategic TDD** — test-first development applied surgically where it delivers the most value. Not everything needs tests; the domains that do are those where silent regressions cause the most damage.
85	
86	### Domain Classification
87	
88	| Level | Domain | Path | Coverage Target | Rationale |
89	|-------|--------|------|----------------|-----------|
90	| **MUST TDD** | Source providers (parsers) | `cli/src/providers/` | 90%+ | External file formats break silently — a provider regression corrupts sync |
91	| **MUST TDD** | Normalizers | `server/src/llm/*-normalize.ts` | 85%+ | 40+ alias mappings; a regression silently corrupts every user's insights |
92	| **MUST TDD** | Analysis pricing | `server/src/llm/analysis-pricing.ts` | 85%+ | Cost calculations are silent — wrong math silently under/overcharges users |
93	| **MUST TDD** | Response parsers | `server/src/llm/response-parsers.ts` | 85%+ | LLM output parsing failures corrupt stored insights silently |
94	| **MUST TDD** | Migrations | `cli/src/db/migrate.ts`, `schema.ts` | 90%+ | Schema changes are irreversible; bugs in migrations can corrupt the database |
95	| **MUST TDD** | Shared utilities | `server/src/utils.ts`, `cli/src/utils/` | 85%+ | Pure functions — trivial to test, used across the codebase |
96	| **SHOULD TDD** | API routes | `server/src/routes/` | 70%+ | High-value but SQLite coupling makes setup harder |
97	| **SKIP TDD** | Dashboard components | `dashboard/src/` | — | Visual, React-rendered — unit tests deliver low value here |
98	| **SKIP TDD** | CLI command wiring | `cli/src/commands/`, `cli/src/index.ts` | — | Integration-level; verify via manual sync runs |
99	
100	### Test-First Workflow (MUST Domains)
101	
102	When changing a MUST TDD domain:
103	
104	1. **Write the failing test first** — before any implementation
105	2. **Run `pnpm test`** — new test should fail (proves it's testing the right thing)
106	3. **Implement until the test passes**
107	4. **Commit test + implementation together** — never split them across commits
108	
109	### Test Patterns
110	
111	| Domain | Pattern | Example file |
112	|--------|---------|-------------|
113	| Normalizers | Table-driven: iterate over `[input, expected]` pairs in one `it()` | `server/src/llm/friction-normalize.test.ts` |
114	| Parsers | Fixture-based: real JSONL content written to temp files | `cli/src/providers/__tests__/claude-code.test.ts` |
115	| Migrations | In-memory SQLite: `new Database(':memory:')` + `runMigrations(db)` | `cli/src/db/__tests__/migrate.test.ts` |
116	| Utilities | Unit tests: pure function input/output, edge cases | `server/src/utils.test.ts` |
117	
118	### Running Tests
119	
120	```bash
121	# From repo root — runs all packages
122	pnpm test
123	
124	# Watch mode for active development
125	pnpm test:watch
126	
127	# Coverage report
128	pnpm test:coverage
129	```
130	
131	Test runner: **vitest** (native ESM support, fast, shared with all packages via root `package.json`).
132	
133	---
134	
135	## Version Bump Procedure
136	
137	When bumping the version (patch, minor, or major):
138	
139	1. **`cli/package.json`** — Update the `"version"` field
140	2. **`cli/CHANGELOG.md`** — Add a new `## [x.y.z] - YYYY-MM-DD` section at the top with changes
141	3. **Commit** — `chore: bump version to vX.Y.Z` with a one-line summary of what changed
142	4. **Publish** — `cd cli && npm publish` (runs `prepublishOnly` which builds all packages)
143	
144	Files touched: `cli/package.json` + `cli/CHANGELOG.md` (minimum). Optionally update `docs/ROADMAP.md`, `docs/VISION.md`, `docs/PRODUCT.md` for minor/major bumps.
145	
146	---
147	
148	## Configuration
149	
150	| File | Purpose |
151	|------|---------|
152	| `~/.code-insights/config.json` | User config (mode 0o600) |
153	| `~/.code-insights/sync-state.json` | File modification tracking for incremental sync |
154	| `~/.code-insights/device-id` | Stable device identifier |
155	| `~/.code-insights/data.db` | SQLite database |
156	
157	### Hook Integration
158	
159	- `install-hook` modifies `~/.claude/settings.json` to add a Stop hook
160	- Hook runs `code-insights sync -q` automatically when Claude Code sessions end
161	
162	---
163	
164	## Development Notes
165	
166	- TypeScript strict mode enabled
167	- ES Modules (`import`/`export`, not `require`)
168	- Test framework: **vitest** — run `pnpm test` from repo root
169	- No ESLint config file in CLI directory (lint script exists but needs config)
170	- pnpm is the package manager (workspace monorepo)
171	- CLI binary is `code-insights`
172	- npm package is `@code-insights/cli`
173

# Development Practices — Code Insights

> Development rules, branch discipline, and operational procedures. Linked from [CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md).

---

## Pre-Action Verification (CRITICAL)

Before any state-modifying command (git checkout, git push, git tag, file edits), run a **read-only check** to verify current state:

- **Branch names:** Never assume `main` vs `master` — run `git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD` to detect the default branch
- **File existence:** Read a file before editing it; `ls` a directory before writing to it
- **API signatures:** When calling an unfamiliar endpoint or tool, read the function signature or documentation first
- **Build context:** Before running build scripts, verify you're in the correct sub-directory (`pwd`) and on the correct branch (`git branch`)

This applies to both **planning** and **execution**.

---

## Retry Discipline

If a command or tool call fails twice on the same input, **STOP**. Do not retry a third time. Report the failure, state what was attempted, and propose an alternative approach.

---

## Branch Discipline (CRITICAL)

`main` is the production branch. Only receives commits via merged PRs.

**Rules for ALL agents:**

```bash
# BEFORE ANY COMMIT:
git branch  # Must show feature branch, NOT main

# If on main -> STOP:
git checkout -b feature/description

# After EVERY commit:
git push origin $(git branch --show-current)  # Push IMMEDIATELY
```

**Branch naming:**
- `feature/description` (new functionality)
- `fix/description` (bug fixes)
- `docs/description` (documentation only)
- `chore/description` (maintenance, deps, config)

**Pre-commit checklist (ALL agents):**
1. `git branch` — Am I on feature branch?
2. If on `main` -> STOP, create feature branch
3. Commit to feature branch
4. Push immediately after commit

---

## Hookify Rules

| Rule | Type | Purpose |
|------|------|---------|
| `block-pr-merge` | **block** | Agents never merge PRs — founder only |
| `block-local-merge-to-main` | **block** | Prevent `git merge` — all merges via GitHub PRs |
| `branch-discipline` | warn | Dev agents verify feature branch before coding |
| `agent-parallel-warning` | warn | Verify no dependencies before parallelizing agents |
| `no-jira` | **block** | Prevent Jira/Atlassian API calls — use GitHub Issues instead |
| `use-git-rm-for-tracked-files` | **block** | Enforce `git rm` over `rm` for source files |
| `review-before-pr` | warn | Remind: code review required before PR creation (bash path) |
| `review-before-pr-mcp` | warn | Remind: code review required before PR creation (MCP path) |
| `verify-before-checkout` | warn | Verify branch exists before `git checkout/switch` |
| `default-subagent-execution` | warn | Auto-answer: default to subagent-driven execution |
| `tdd-domain-check` | warn | On `git commit`, remind to include tests when touching TDD domains |

**Native PreToolUse hooks** (in `settings.json`, not hookify):

| Trigger | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `gh pr create` (Bash) | Run `pnpm build && pnpm test` — blocks PR creation on failure |
| `create_pull_request` (MCP) | Same CI gate for GitHub MCP tool path |

---

## TDD Strategy

Code Insights uses **strategic TDD** — test-first development applied surgically where it delivers the most value. Not everything needs tests; the domains that do are those where silent regressions cause the most damage.

### Domain Classification

| Level | Domain | Path | Coverage Target | Rationale |
|-------|--------|------|----------------|-----------|
| **MUST TDD** | Source providers (parsers) | `cli/src/providers/` | 90%+ | External file formats break silently — a provider regression corrupts sync |
| **MUST TDD** | Normalizers | `server/src/llm/*-normalize.ts` | 85%+ | 40+ alias mappings; a regression silently corrupts every user's insights |
| **MUST TDD** | Analysis pricing | `server/src/llm/analysis-pricing.ts` | 85%+ | Cost calculations are silent — wrong math silently under/overcharges users |
| **MUST TDD** | Response parsers | `server/src/llm/response-parsers.ts` | 85%+ | LLM output parsing failures corrupt stored insights silently |
| **MUST TDD** | Migrations | `cli/src/db/migrate.ts`, `schema.ts` | 90%+ | Schema changes are irreversible; bugs in migrations can corrupt the database |
| **MUST TDD** | Shared utilities | `server/src/utils.ts`, `cli/src/utils/` | 85%+ | Pure functions — trivial to test, used across the codebase |
| **SHOULD TDD** | API routes | `server/src/routes/` | 70%+ | High-value but SQLite coupling makes setup harder |
| **SKIP TDD** | Dashboard components | `dashboard/src/` | — | Visual, React-rendered — unit tests deliver low value here |
| **SKIP TDD** | CLI command wiring | `cli/src/commands/`, `cli/src/index.ts` | — | Integration-level; verify via manual sync runs |

### Test-First Workflow (MUST Domains)

When changing a MUST TDD domain:

1. **Write the failing test first** — before any implementation
2. **Run `pnpm test`** — new test should fail (proves it's testing the right thing)
3. **Implement until the test passes**
4. **Commit test + implementation together** — never split them across commits

### Test Patterns

| Domain | Pattern | Example file |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| Normalizers | Table-driven: iterate over `[input, expected]` pairs in one `it()` | `server/src/llm/friction-normalize.test.ts` |
| Parsers | Fixture-based: real JSONL content written to temp files | `cli/src/providers/__tests__/claude-code.test.ts` |
| Migrations | In-memory SQLite: `new Database(':memory:')` + `runMigrations(db)` | `cli/src/db/__tests__/migrate.test.ts` |
| Utilities | Unit tests: pure function input/output, edge cases | `server/src/utils.test.ts` |

### Running Tests

```bash
# From repo root — runs all packages
pnpm test

# Watch mode for active development
pnpm test:watch

# Coverage report
pnpm test:coverage
```

Test runner: **vitest** (native ESM support, fast, shared with all packages via root `package.json`).

---

## Version Bump Procedure

When bumping the version (patch, minor, or major):

1. **`cli/package.json`** — Update the `"version"` field
2. **`cli/CHANGELOG.md`** — Add a new `## [x.y.z] - YYYY-MM-DD` section at the top with changes
3. **Commit** — `chore: bump version to vX.Y.Z` with a one-line summary of what changed
4. **Publish** — `cd cli && npm publish` (runs `prepublishOnly` which builds all packages)

Files touched: `cli/package.json` + `cli/CHANGELOG.md` (minimum). Optionally update `docs/ROADMAP.md`, `docs/VISION.md`, `docs/PRODUCT.md` for minor/major bumps.

---

## Configuration

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `~/.code-insights/config.json` | User config (mode 0o600) |
| `~/.code-insights/sync-state.json` | File modification tracking for incremental sync |
| `~/.code-insights/device-id` | Stable device identifier |
| `~/.code-insights/data.db` | SQLite database |

### Hook Integration

- `install-hook` modifies `~/.claude/settings.json` to add a Stop hook
- Hook runs `code-insights sync -q` automatically when Claude Code sessions end

---

## Development Notes

- TypeScript strict mode enabled
- ES Modules (`import`/`export`, not `require`)
- Test framework: **vitest** — run `pnpm test` from repo root
- No ESLint config file in CLI directory (lint script exists but needs config)
- pnpm is the package manager (workspace monorepo)
- CLI binary is `code-insights`
- npm package is `@code-insights/cli`

# Gamification & Shareable Badges — Design Plan

**Date:** 2026-03-08
**Status:** Future feature (design phase — not yet approved for implementation)
**Prerequisite:** Patterns Page Refinement (2026-03-08-patterns-page-refinement.md)
**Depends on:** Working Style hero card, tagline, streak computation

---

## Vision

Enable users to download shareable badge/image cards from Code Insights that they can post on LinkedIn, X/Twitter, and other social media. This serves as an organic growth channel — users share their "developer identity card" or "coding stats" and it drives awareness of Code Insights.

**Precedent:** GitHub Wrapped, Spotify Wrapped, WakaTime badges, GitPodcast. The "developer personality type" angle (like MBTI for coding) is the unique differentiator.

---

## Strategic Assessment (DevTools Cofounder)

### Why This Works
- Developer identity content gets shared (GitHub Wrapped proved this at scale)
- "AI coding session analysis" is a novel angle no competitor has
- The archetype tagline ("The Methodical Builder") is inherently share-worthy — it's identity-forming
- Multi-tool angle is unique: "I use 4 AI coding tools" is a flex developers want to make

### Risks
- **Gimmicky perception:** Mitigate by framing as "professional development insight" not "fun badge"
- **Low sharing rate:** Most developer tools see <5% share rate. That's fine — even 2% of users sharing generates organic reach
- **Privacy anxiety:** Users must feel 100% confident no sensitive data leaks into the card

### Growth Mechanics
- Card includes `code-insights.app` URL for attribution
- Consider a landing page explaining archetypes ("What's your coding style?")
- Comparison angle: "I'm a Methodical Builder — what are you?"
- Potential hashtag: `#MyCodeStyle` or `#CodeInsights`

---

## Shareable Surfaces (Phased)

### Phase 1: Working Style Card (Patterns Page)
The hero card from the Patterns page becomes the first shareable image.

### Phase 2: Stats Card (Stats Page) — Future
Monthly/period stats summary with key metrics.

### Phase 3: Milestone Cards — Future
Achievement-triggered cards (100 sessions, 30-day streak, etc.)

---

## Technical Architecture

### Image Generation: `html-to-image`

| Aspect | Decision |
|--------|----------|
| Library | `html-to-image` (~12KB gzip) |
| Approach | Purpose-built card component rendered off-screen, not a page screenshot |
| Format | PNG only (universal compatibility) |

# Growth Priorities Plan

> Strategic plan from devtools-cofounder analysis (2026-03-18). Execution tracked below.

---

## Strategic Context

Code Insights has **deep product** but **narrow distribution**. ~2,600 npm downloads/month, 6 GitHub stars. The analytical depth (9 friction categories, 8 pattern categories, attribution models, prompt quality scoring) rivals tools with full teams. The gap is between capability and awareness.

**Core thesis:** Build wider reach before deeper product.

---

## Priority Order

### Priority 1: Zero-Config First-Run Experience -- SHIPPED (v4.1.0)

**Goal:** `npx @code-insights/cli` gives value in 30 seconds with zero config.

**What shipped:**
- `code-insights` (no args) runs sync + opens dashboard automatically (PR #195)
- `dashboard` command auto-syncs before starting, `--no-sync` to skip (PR #195)
- InsightsPage empty state guides to LLM config as "unlock" not wall (PR #195)
- `identifyUser()` called on first-run default path (review fix)
- All docs updated across both repos (PR #197 code-insights, PR #129 code-insights-web)
- Marketing site "How It Works" updated: "One command. No accounts. No configuration."
- SEO structured data (JSON-LD) updated for Google rich snippets
- Released as v4.1.0 on npm

**Key design decisions:**
- `init` is optional, not removed -- deprioritized in messaging
- `dashboard` remains a standalone documented command (starts Hono server)
- `stats` is terminal-only, doesn't need dashboard
- `quiet: false` on auto-sync so first-run users see progress
- Sync failure is non-fatal -- dashboard opens with whatever data exists

---

### Priority 3: Shareable Badges -- NEXT

**Goal:** Viral loop: see badge on social media -> visit code-insights.app -> `npx @code-insights/cli` -> own dashboard in 30s -> generate own badge -> share.

**Existing plan:** `docs/plans/gamification-shareable-badges.md` (read this first -- comprehensive spec)

**Phase 1 scope (what to build now):**

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `WorkingStyleShareCard` | Purpose-built 1200x630 card, hardcoded hex colors, system fonts, inline SVG donut chart |
| `html-to-image` integration | ~12KB dep, renders off-screen, PNG export at 2x pixel ratio |
| Download button | On existing `WeekAtAGlanceStrip` hero card (top-right, visible after Reflect generation) |
| Data on card | Archetype tagline, session count, streak, tools used, character distribution donut |
| Privacy | No project names, file paths, LLM narrative, git branches, API keys, usernames |

**Technical details:**
- Fixed dimensions: 1200x630px (1.91:1 ratio -- works on LinkedIn and X/Twitter)
- Dark gradient theme: `linear-gradient(135deg, #0f0f23, #1a1a3e)` + radial glows
- Page hero card uses Tailwind/CSS variables; shareable card uses hardcoded values for export reliability
- Inline SVG donut chart (not Recharts -- canvas compatibility)
- `html-to-image` library (~12KB gzip) for PNG conversion
- Browser-triggered file download: `code-insights-working-style.png`
- No preview modal -- hero card IS the WYSIWYG preview

**Computed milestones (no new DB tables):**

| Milestone | Trigger | Display |
|-----------|---------|---------|
| Session count | 50, 100, 250, 500, 1000 | "500 sessions" badge |
| Active streak | 7, 14, 30, 60, 90 days | Flame icon + duration |
| Multi-tool | 2+, 3+, 4 source tools | Tool icons + count |
| Analysis coverage | >80% analyzed | "Deep Analyzer" label |
| Success rate | >85% over 30+ sessions | "High Success" indicator |

**Dependencies:**
- `tagline` field in `WorkingStyleResult` type (check if Patterns refinement added this)
- Hero card component `WeekAtAGlanceStrip` (already exists)
- Streak computation (already exists in aggregation layer)

**Phase 2 (future):** Stats card variant, milestone markers

input.prompt:
I need your strategic assessment of what Code Insights should build next. Please read the docs carefully and give me a clear prioritized recommendation.

## What Code Insights Is

Free, open-source, local-first developer tool. Parses AI coding sessions (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Copilot CLI, VS Code Copilot Chat) into a local SQLite DB. Provides a local Vite+React dashboard and CLI analytics. No cloud, no accounts, no monetization.

**Current version:** v4.9.4 (npm: `@code-insights/cli`)
**Stack:** Node.js CLI + Hono API server + Vite+React SPA, SQLite

---

## Where The Product Is Right Now

### Completed Phases

| Phase | What shipped |
|-------|-------------|
| 1–6 | Foundation, integration, LLM analysis, multi-source (5 tools), telemetry, npm distribution |
| 7 | Export & Knowledge Pipeline (session export templates, cross-session LLM synthesis, 4 formats) |
| 8–8.5 | Reflect & Patterns (9 friction categories + attribution, 8 pattern categories + driver, ISO week navigation, CoT reasoning) |
| 9 | Infrastructure (message classification V6, prompt caching, cost tracking V7) |
| 10 | UX & Shareability (zero-config first run, AI Fluency Score card 1200×630 PNG, PQ dimension scores) |
| 11 | Search, Filtering & Reliability (Cmd+K command palette, advanced filters, ErrorBoundary) |
| 12 | **Native Analysis via Claude Code hooks** (zero-config session analysis using Claude subscription, `insights --native`, V8 schema) |

### The "What's Next" Section in Docs

The ROADMAP.md says:
```
- Progress tracking: weekly snapshots, friction-to-pattern affinity map, transformation detection, driver-based filtering for user growth signals
- Test suite expansion (Vitest)
- Session merging across tools
- Shareable badges Phase 2: stats card variant, milestone-specific cards
```

VISION.md also calls out progress tracking as the next major layer:
> Progress tracking — "Am I getting better?" Weekly snapshots comparing friction trends and pattern emergence over time, helping developers see how their AI collaboration skills evolve.

### Growth Numbers (from growth plan doc, ~3 weeks old)
- ~2,600 npm downloads/month
- 6 GitHub stars
- Core observation: "deep product but narrow distribution"

---

## The Doc/Implementation Disconnect

The user flagged a possible disconnect between docs and implementation. Here's what I've found:

1. **docs/plans/ has stale plan files** — Many plans from Feb-March 2026 sit in the directory even after their PRs merged. The MEMORY.md says "12 stale plan files deleted" but looking at the directory now there are still ~18 plan files, many for already-shipped features (message classification, prompt caching, cost tracking, share card canvas rewrite, etc.).

2. **Schema version mismatch in CLAUDE.md** — CLAUDE.md still says "V8 planned for native analysis" but native analysis SHIPPED (Phase 12, PR #243). The schema should already be at V8. The ARCHITECTURE.md still shows the table as V7.

3. **PRODUCT.md** lists "Schema V7" in tech stack but the native analysis (Phase 12) introduced V8 schema.

4. **The growth priorities plan (docs/plans/2026-03-19-growth-priorities-plan.md)** lists Priority 3 as "Shareable Badges — NEXT" but those shipped in Phase 10 (v4.2.0, v4.3.0). The plan hasn't been updated to reflect what shipped.

---

## Key Product Context

- **Friction taxonomy (9 categories):** wrong-approach, knowledge-gap, stale-assumptions, incomplete-requirements, context-loss, scope-creep, repeated-mistakes, documentation-gap, tooling-limitation
- **Pattern taxonomy (8 categories):** structured-planning, incremental-implementation, verification-workflow, systematic-debugging, self-correction, context-gathering, domain-expertise, effective-tooling
- **Attribution model:** friction points are `user-actionable | ai-capability | environmental`
- **Driver model:** patterns are `user-driven | ai-driven | collaborative`
- **Progress tracking design** (in docs/plans/): snapshot-vs-snapshot comparison, friction-to-pattern affinity map, 3 weekly snapshots minimum, user-actionable friction as primary growth signal, lightweight LLM narrative (Haiku)
- **Weekly Reflect** already ships snapshot caching, ISO week navigation, and the underlying facet data structures that progress tracking would build on

---

## My Questions for You

1. **What should we build next** — given v4.9.4 is shipped and Phase 12 is complete? Is "Progress tracking" the right bet, or is there a higher-leverage thing to tackle first? Consider: we have 2,600 downloads/month but 6 GitHub stars, suggesting adoption without engagement/community.

2. **Doc cleanup priority** — Should we clean up the stale plan files and fix schema version references before the next feature? Or ship the feature and clean up after?

3. **Distribution vs depth** — The growth plan said "build wider reach before deeper product." Progress tracking is a deeper product feature. Is that still the right call, or should we be doing something to drive distribution/awareness first?

4. **One concrete recommendation** — Given everything above, what is the single highest-leverage next move?

Please be specific. Reference the features described above where relevant. Avoid generic growth advice.

content.text:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me write the strategic assessment.

---

## Strategic Assessment: What Code Insights Should Build Next

### The Numbers Have Changed — And That Matters

The growth plan from March 19 said "6 GitHub stars." You now have **38 stars and 4 forks**. That is a 6x jump in under 3 weeks. Something is working. Before deciding what to build, you need to understand what drove that. Was it an HN post? A tweet? Someone sharing a badge? Organic discovery? The answer shapes whether you double down on distribution or shift to retention.

**First action item:** Check your PostHog dashboard and GitHub traffic (Settings > Traffic) to understand what drove the star spike. If you do not know why users showed up, you cannot intentionally make more of them show up.

---

### Market Context

The AI coding analytics space is getting real. ccusage (open-source Claude Code cost tracker) has been getting traction on GitHub. Haiku.inc is building session replay. Pieces is doing local-first context. But none of them have **cross-tool analysis** — that remains your unique positioning. The fact that you parse 5 different tools while everyone else is single-tool is genuinely differentiated.

The native analysis feature (Phase 12) was strategically correct — it removed the single biggest friction point (requiring users to bring their own API key). That was the right call. Now the question is: what do users DO after they have sessions synced and analyzed?

---

### Answering Your Four Questions

#### 1. Should you build Progress Tracking next?

**Not yet.** Here is why.

Progress tracking ("Am I getting better?") requires users to have **3-4 weekly snapshots minimum** before they see any value. That means a new user who installs today will not get value from Progress for a month. With 38 stars and early traction, your current challenge is **activation and retention in week 1**, not longitudinal engagement in month 2.

Progress tracking is a retention feature for power users. You do not have enough power users yet to justify building it. The growth plan correctly ranked it Priority 4 (last). That ranking is still correct.

**What should you build instead?**

The highest-leverage move right now is **distribution mechanics** — specifically, the things that turn your 38-star moment into a 200-star moment. I will be concrete below.

#### 2. Doc cleanup priority

**Do it now, before the next feature. It is cheap and high-ROI.**

Here is why this matters more than it seems: you have 4 forks. People are reading your code. Stale plan files, wrong schema versions in CLAUDE.md, and shipped features still marked "NEXT" in the growth plan — these signal "abandoned project" to contributors evaluating whether to invest time. For an open-source project at your stage, perceived project health IS your moat.

Specific cleanup:
- Delete the ~15 stale plan files for shipped features (keep only gamification-shareable-badges.md and the progress tracking section in pattern-normalization.md)
- Fix CLAUDE.md: "V8 planned" should reflect V8 shipped
- Fix ARCHITECTURE.md: Schema V7 references should show V8
- Update growth-priorities-plan.md: mark shareable badges as SHIPPED
- Fix Phase 12 in ROADMAP.md: still says "(Planned)" in the section header even though all milestones are checked

This is a 1-2 hour task. It makes the project look maintained and intentional. Ship it as a `docs: clean up stale plans and fix schema version references` commit.

#### 3. Distribution vs Depth

**Distribution. Emphatically.**

The growth plan's thesis — "build wider reach before deeper product" — is even MORE correct now than when it was written. You went from 6 to 38 stars organically. You have product-market resonance with a small audience. The product is deep enough. What you need is reach.

Here is the uncomfortable truth about developer tools: **a tool with mediocre features and great distribution beats a tool with great features and no distribution.** You have great features. WakaTime is a B+ product with an A+ distribution engine — README badges, IDE integrations, leaderboards. You are an A product that most developers have never heard of.

The Obsidian analogy from your competitive landscape is instructive. Obsidian did not win because they had the deepest note-taking features on day 1. They won because: (a) zero-config local-first resonated, (b) the community plugin system created network effects, and (c) every user became a distributor through shared vaults, themes, and workflow screenshots. You already have (a). You cannot build (c) with 38 stars. So focus on getting to 200+ stars where community effects start compounding.

#### 4. The Single Highest-Leverage Next Move

**A Hacker News "Show HN" launch, with the README and landing page polished for that moment.**

Here is my reasoning:

- Your product already has the substance. Five source tools, local-first, native analysis with zero config, shareable cards, friction taxonomy, pattern detection. This is a real tool, not a weekend project.
- Your README is solid but missing the "hero GIF" — the 15-second recording that shows sync-to-dashboard. The growth plan called this out. It is still not done.
- HN loves: open-source, local-first, privacy-respecting, cross-tool, and "I built this solo." You check every box.
- The native analysis feature (no API key needed for Claude Code users) is a strong HN hook. "Analyze your AI coding sessions with zero config" is a compelling one-liner.
- 38 stars means you have enough social proof to not look brand new, but not so many that a Show HN feels late.

**Pre-launch checklist (what to build/fix before posting):**

| Item | Effort | Why It Matters |
|------|--------|----------------|
| README hero GIF (15s: npx -> sync -> dashboard) | S | HN readers decide in 10 seconds. A GIF is worth 1000 words of README. |
| Doc cleanup (stale plans, schema versions) | S | Forks will look at docs. Clean house before guests arrive. |
| Verify `npx @code-insights/cli` cold-start path | S | Every HN reader will run this. It must work flawlessly on macOS and Linux with zero config. Test on a clean machine. |
| README "What you get" section with 3-4 dashboard screenshots | S | Show the dashboard, not just describe it. Light and dark mode. |
| CONTRIBUTING.md review | S | HN traffic converts to stars. Stars convert to contributors only if onboarding is clear. |

**What NOT to build before launch:**
- Progress tracking (too early, adds complexity)
- Shareable badges Phase 2 (Phase 1 is enough)
- Session merging (nice-to-have, not a launch hook)
- Test suite expansion (important but invisible to users)

---

### After the Launch: What Comes Next

Assuming the HN launch goes well and you get to 100-200+ stars:

1. **Week 1-2 post-launch:** Fix whatever breaks. Monitor PostHog. Respond to GitHub issues. This is when contributor energy is highest — make it easy to contribute.

2. **Then:** Progress tracking becomes the right move. By then, early adopters from the launch will have 3-4 weeks of data. They will be asking "am I getting better?" organically. Build it when users pull for it, not when you push it on them.

3. **Parallel:** Test suite expansion. As contributors appear, you need test coverage to accept PRs safely. This is infrastructure that enables community, not product depth.

---

### Risks

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|
| HN post flops | The product still improves from the prep work. README hero GIF and doc cleanup benefit all future discovery channels. |
| HN traffic crashes the npx cold start | Test on clean machines. The sync + dashboard flow must handle 0 sessions gracefully (you already have empty states from Phase 10). |
| Feature requests flood in post-launch | Triage ruthlessly. "Thank you, filed as an issue" is a valid response. Do not scope-creep based on HN comments. |
| Someone posts "just use ccusage" | Different product. ccusage is cost tracking only. You are cross-tool session analysis with friction/pattern detection. The overlap is superficial. |

---

### Summary

| Question | Answer |
|----------|--------|
| What to build next? | Not a feature. A launch. Polish README + hero GIF + doc cleanup, then Show HN. |
| Doc cleanup priority? | Now. Before anything else. 1-2 hours, high ROI for perceived project health. |
| Distribution vs depth? | Distribution. The product is deep enough. Reach is the bottleneck. |
| Single highest-leverage move? | Show HN launch with polished README (hero GIF, screenshots, clean docs). |
| When to build Progress tracking? | After the launch, when early adopters have 3-4 weeks of data and ask for it. |

The product is ready. The docs are not. Fix the docs, record the GIF, and ship the launch.

I need your strategic assessment of what Code Insights should build next. Please read the docs carefully and give me a clear prioritized recommendation.

## What Code Insights Is

Free, open-source, local-first developer tool. Parses AI coding sessions (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Copilot CLI, VS Code Copilot Chat) into a local SQLite DB. Provides a local Vite+React dashboard and CLI analytics. No cloud, no accounts, no monetization.

**Current version:** v4.9.4 (npm: `@code-insights/cli`)
**Stack:** Node.js CLI + Hono API server + Vite+React SPA, SQLite

---

## Where The Product Is Right Now

### Completed Phases

| Phase | What shipped |
|-------|-------------|
| 1–6 | Foundation, integration, LLM analysis, multi-source (5 tools), telemetry, npm distribution |
| 7 | Export & Knowledge Pipeline (session export templates, cross-session LLM synthesis, 4 formats) |
| 8–8.5 | Reflect & Patterns (9 friction categories + attribution, 8 pattern categories + driver, ISO week navigation, CoT reasoning) |
| 9 | Infrastructure (message classification V6, prompt caching, cost tracking V7) |
| 10 | UX & Shareability (zero-config first run, AI Fluency Score card 1200×630 PNG, PQ dimension scores) |
| 11 | Search, Filtering & Reliability (Cmd+K command palette, advanced filters, ErrorBoundary) |
| 12 | **Native Analysis via Claude Code hooks** (zero-config session analysis using Claude subscription, `insights --native`, V8 schema) |

### The "What's Next" Section in Docs

The ROADMAP.md says:
```
- Progress tracking: weekly snapshots, friction-to-pattern affinity map, transformation detection, driver-based filtering for user growth signals
- Test suite expansion (Vitest)
- Session merging across tools
- Shareable badges Phase 2: stats card variant, milestone-specific cards
```

VISION.md also calls out progress tracking as the next major layer:
> Progress tracking — "Am I getting better?" Weekly snapshots comparing friction trends and pattern emergence over time, helping developers see how their AI collaboration skills evolve.

### Growth Numbers (from growth plan doc, ~3 weeks old)
- ~2,600 npm downloads/month
- 6 GitHub stars
- Core observation: "deep product but narrow distribution"

---

## The Doc/Implementation Disconnect

The user flagged a possible disconnect between docs and implementation. Here's what I've found:

1. **docs/plans/ has stale plan files** — Many plans from Feb-March 2026 sit in the directory even after their PRs merged. The MEMORY.md says "12 stale plan files deleted" but looking at the directory now there are still ~18 plan files, many for already-shipped features (message classification, prompt caching, cost tracking, share card canvas rewrite, etc.).

Now I have a complete picture. Let me write the strategic assessment.

---

## Strategic Assessment: What Code Insights Should Build Next

### The Numbers Have Changed — And That Matters

The growth plan from March 19 said "6 GitHub stars." You now have **38 stars and 4 forks**. That is a 6x jump in under 3 weeks. Something is working. Before deciding what to build, you need to understand what drove that. Was it an HN post? A tweet? Someone sharing a badge? Organic discovery? The answer shapes whether you double down on distribution or shift to retention.

**First action item:** Check your PostHog dashboard and GitHub traffic (Settings > Traffic) to understand what drove the star spike. If you do not know why users showed up, you cannot intentionally make more of them show up.

---

### Market Context

The AI coding analytics space is getting real. ccusage (open-source Claude Code cost tracker) has been getting traction on GitHub. Haiku.inc is building session replay. Pieces is doing local-first context. But none of them have **cross-tool analysis** — that remains your unique positioning. The fact that you parse 5 different tools while everyone else is single-tool is genuinely differentiated.

The native analysis feature (Phase 12) was strategically correct — it removed the single biggest friction point (requiring users to bring their own API key). That was the right call. Now the question is: what do users DO after they have sessions synced and analyzed?

---

### Answering Your Four Questions

#### 1. Should you build Progress Tracking next?

**Not yet.** Here is why.

Progress tracking ("Am I getting better?") requires users to have **3-4 weekly snapshots minimum** before they see any value. That means a new user who installs today will not get value from Progress for a month. With 38 stars and early traction, your current challenge is **activation and retention in week 1**, not longitudinal engagement in month 2.

Progress tracking is a retention feature for power users. You do not have enough power users yet to justify building it. The growth plan correctly ranked it Priority 4 (last). That ranking is still correct.

**What should you build instead?**

The highest-leverage move right now is **distribution mechanics** — specifically, the things that turn your 38-star moment into a 200-star moment. I will be concrete below.

#### 2. Doc cleanup priority

**Do it now, before the next feature. It is cheap and high-ROI.**

Here is why this matters more than it seems: you have 4 forks. People are reading your code. Stale plan files, wrong schema versions in CLAUDE.md, and shipped features still marked "NEXT" in the growth plan — these signal "abandoned project" to contributors evaluating whether to invest time. For an open-source project at your stage, perceived project health IS your moat.

Specific cleanup:
- Delete the ~15 stale plan files for shipped features (keep only gamification-shareable-badges.md and the progress tracking section in pattern-normalization.md)
- Fix CLAUDE.md: "V8 planned" should reflect V8 shipped
- Fix ARCHITECTURE.md: Schema V7 references should show V8
- Update growth-priorities-plan.md: mark shareable badges as SHIPPED
- Fix Phase 12 in ROADMAP.md: still says "(Planned)" in the section header even though all milestones are checked

This is a 1-2 hour task. It makes the project look maintained and intentional. Ship it as a `docs: clean up stale plans and fix schema version references` commit.

#### 3. Distribution vs Depth

**Distribution. Emphatically.**

The growth plan's thesis — "build wider reach before deeper product" — is even MORE correct now than when it was written. You went from 6 to 38 stars organically. You have product-market resonance with a small audience. The product is deep enough. What you need is reach.

Here is the uncomfortable truth about developer tools: **a tool with mediocre features and great distribution beats a tool with great features and no distribution.** You have great features. WakaTime is a B+ product with an A+ distribution engine — README badges, IDE integrations, leaderboards. You are an A product that most developers have never heard of.

The Obsidian analogy from your competitive landscape is instructive. Obsidian did not win because they had the deepest note-taking features on day 1. They won because: (a) zero-config local-first resonated, (b) the community plugin system created network effects, and (c) every user became a distributor through shared vaults, themes, and workflow screenshots. You already have (a). You cannot build (c) with 38 stars. So focus on getting to 200+ stars where community effects start compounding.

#### 4. The Single Highest-Leverage Next Move

**A Hacker News "Show HN" launch, with the README and landing page polished for that moment.**

Here is my reasoning:

- Your product already has the substance. Five source tools, local-first, native analysis with zero config, shareable cards, friction taxonomy, pattern detection. This is a real tool, not a weekend project.
- Your README is solid but missing the "hero GIF" — the 15-second recording that shows sync-to-dashboard. The growth plan called this out. It is still not done.
- HN loves: open-source, local-first, privacy-respecting, cross-tool, and "I built this solo." You check every box.
- The native analysis feature (no API key needed for Claude Code users) is a strong HN hook. "Analyze your AI coding sessions with zero config" is a compelling one-liner.
- 38 stars means you have enough social proof to not look brand new, but not so many that a Show HN feels late.

**Pre-launch checklist (what to build/fix before posting):**

| Item | Effort | Why It Matters |
|------|--------|----------------|
| README hero GIF (15s: npx -> sync -> dashboard) | S | HN readers decide in 10 seconds. A GIF is worth 1000 words of README. |
| Doc cleanup (stale plans, schema versions) | S | Forks will look at docs. Clean house before guests arrive. |
| Verify `npx @code-insights/cli` cold-start path | S | Every HN reader will run this. It must work flawlessly on macOS and Linux with zero config. Test on a clean machine. |
| README "What you get" section with 3-4 dashboard screenshots | S | Show the dashboard, not just describe it. Light and dark mode. |
| CONTRIBUTING.md review | S | HN traffic converts to stars. Stars convert to contributors only if onboarding is clear. |

**What NOT to build before launch:**
- Progress tracking (too early, adds complexity)
- Shareable badges Phase 2 (Phase 1 is enough)
- Session merging (nice-to-have, not a launch hook)
- Test suite expansion (important but invisible to users)

---

### After the Launch: What Comes Next

Assuming the HN launch goes well and you get to 100-200+ stars:

1. **Week 1-2 post-launch:** Fix whatever breaks. Monitor PostHog. Respond to GitHub issues. This is when contributor energy is highest — make it easy to contribute.

2. **Then:** Progress tracking becomes the right move. By then, early adopters from the launch will have 3-4 weeks of data. They will be asking "am I getting better?" organically. Build it when users pull for it, not when you push it on them.

3. **Parallel:** Test suite expansion. As contributors appear, you need test coverage to accept PRs safely. This is infrastructure that enables community, not product depth.

---

### Risks

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|
| HN post flops | The product still improves from the prep work. README hero GIF and doc cleanup benefit all future discovery channels. |
| HN traffic crashes the npx cold start | Test on clean machines. The sync + dashboard flow must handle 0 sessions gracefully (you already have empty states from Phase 10). |
| Feature requests flood in post-launch | Triage ruthlessly. "Thank you, filed as an issue" is a valid response. Do not scope-creep based on HN comments. |
| Someone posts "just use ccusage" | Different product. ccusage is cost tracking only. You are cross-tool session analysis with friction/pattern detection. The overlap is superficial. |

---

### Summary

| Question | Answer |
|----------|--------|
| What to build next? | Not a feature. A launch. Polish README + hero GIF + doc cleanup, then Show HN. |
| Doc cleanup priority? | Now. Before anything else. 1-2 hours, high ROI for perceived project health. |
| Distribution vs depth? | Distribution. The product is deep enough. Reach is the bottleneck. |
| Single highest-leverage move? | Show HN launch with polished README (hero GIF, screenshots, clean docs). |
| When to build Progress tracking? | After the launch, when early adopters have 3-4 weeks of data and ask for it. |

The product is ready. The docs are not. Fix the docs, record the GIF, and ship the launch.

This is exactly the kind of doc/reality drift that compounds — stale docs make the project look abandoned to potential contributors evaluating forks.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Here's the devtools-cofounder's full assessment:

---

## Strategic Assessment

### The Core Finding

**The product is ready. The docs and distribution are not.** Don't build a new feature yet.

---

### What to Do Next (Prioritized)

#### 1. Doc cleanup first — 1-2 hours, high ROI

The user flagged a disconnect, and it's real. Specific issues:

| Problem | Location |
|---------|----------|
| Schema still shows "V7" / "V8 planned" | CLAUDE.md, ARCHITECTURE.md |
| Phase 12 header still says "(Planned)" | ROADMAP.md (all milestones are ✅) |

This matters because the project has **4 forks** — people are reading the code and docs. Stale plans signal "abandoned project."

#### 2. A "Show HN" launch — not a new feature

- **Pre-launch checklist:** hero GIF, README screenshots (light+dark dashboard), verify cold-start `npx @code-insights/cli` on a clean machine, CONTRIBUTING.md review

#### 3. Do NOT build Progress Tracking yet

Progress tracking requires 3-4 weekly snapshots before a user sees value. **That's a month-1 feature, not a day-1 feature.** Your current challenge is week-1 activation, not month-2 retention. Build it after the HN launch, when early adopters actually have the data and are asking for it.

---

### On Distribution vs Depth

> "The product is deep enough. WakaTime is a B+ product with an A+ distribution engine. You are an A product that most developers have never heard of."

The thesis from the March growth plan — "build wider reach before deeper product" — is **still correct** and even more correct now given the star spike.

---

### Recommended Order

1. **Now:** Doc cleanup (schema versions, stale plans, Phase 12 status)
2. **Then:** README hero GIF + dashboard screenshots  
3. **Then:** Show HN post
4. **Post-launch:** Progress tracking (when early adopters ask for it organically)
